text: The best among you are those who have the best manners and character.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Seek knowledge from the cradle to the grave.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The strong is not the one who overcomes people by his strength, but the one who controls himself while in anger.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Make things easy, do not make them difficult. Give good tidings, do not drive people away.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The most beloved of deeds to Allah are those that are most consistent, even if they are small.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: A kind word is a form of charity.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: He who does not show mercy to others, will not be shown mercy.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The best of people are those most beneficial to people.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him speak good or remain silent.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Do not belittle any good deed, even meeting your brother with a cheerful face.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The ink of the scholar is more sacred than the blood of the martyr.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Patience is a pillar of faith.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Leave that which makes you doubt for that which does not make you doubt.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Modesty brings nothing but good.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The best richness is the richness of the soul.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Part of the perfection of a person's Islam is leaving that which does not concern him.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Whoever follows a path in pursuit of knowledge, Allah will make easy for him a path to Paradise.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The world is a prison for the believer and a paradise for the disbeliever.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Verily, actions are judged by intentions.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Give the worker his wages before his sweat dries.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The most perfect believer in faith is the one whose character is finest.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Beware of envy, for envy devours good deeds as fire devours wood.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: He is not a believer whose stomach is filled while his neighbor goes hungry.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Tie your camel and then put your trust in Allah.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Richness is not having many possessions, but richness is being content with oneself.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The best charity is that given in Ramadan.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Be in this world as if you were a stranger or a traveler.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Allah does not look at your forms and possessions, but He looks at your hearts and deeds.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Feed the hungry, visit the sick, and set free the captives.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: When you see a person who has been given more than you in money and beauty, look to those who have been given less.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Speak the truth even if it is bitter.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The most beloved places to Allah are the mosques, and the most hated places to Allah are the markets.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Do not waste water even if you are at a running stream.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Whoever is not grateful to people is not grateful to Allah.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The best of you are those who learn the Quran and teach it.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: A Muslim is the one from whose tongue and hand the people are safe.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Smiling in the face of your brother is charity.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The most hated permissible thing in the sight of Allah is divorce.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Whoever does not abandon falsehood in word and action, Allah has no need that he should leave his food and drink.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Take advantage of five before five: your youth before your old age, your health before your sickness, your wealth before your poverty, your free time before your busyness, and your life before your death.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The one who guides to something good has a reward similar to the one who does it.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Allah is gentle and loves gentleness in all affairs.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: There is a polish for everything that takes away rust, and the polish of the heart is the remembrance of Allah.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The most complete of the believers in faith are those with the best character.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Beware of suspicion, for suspicion is the worst of false tales.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: He who is not merciful to our young and does not respect our old is not one of us.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The best jihad is to speak a word of truth to an oppressive ruler.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Removing a harmful thing from the road is charity.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Verily, with hardship comes ease.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: A believer to another believer is like a building whose parts reinforce each other.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The supplication is the essence of worship.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Whoever treads a path seeking knowledge, Allah will make easy for him the path to Paradise.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Exchange gifts, for they remove grudges from the heart.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The best remembrance is there is no god but Allah, and the best supplication is all praise is for Allah.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The Muslim does not slander, curse, speak obscenely, or speak rudely.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: He who eats his fill while his neighbor goes without food is not a believer.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Every act of kindness is charity.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Deliberation is from Allah, and haste is from Satan.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Whoever humbles himself for the sake of Allah, Allah will raise him.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The best of deeds is to bring happiness to a fellow Muslim.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: A good word is charity.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Do not be people without minds of your own, saying if others treat you well you will treat them well. Rather, accustom yourselves to do good if people do good and not to do wrong if they do evil.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Whoever is offered fragrance should not refuse it, for it is light to carry and pleasant in scent.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The dunya is cursed. Cursed is all that is in it except the remembrance of Allah.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Make use of five before five: your youth before old age, your health before sickness, your riches before poverty, your leisure before work, and your life before death.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: I was only sent to perfect noble character.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Allah loves kindness in all matters.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The two feet of the son of Adam will not move on the Day of Judgment until he is asked about his life and how he spent it.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: When Allah loves a servant, He tests him.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: A person is upon the religion of his closest friend, so let one of you look at whom he befriends.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Spread peace, feed the hungry, pray at night when people are sleeping, and you will enter Paradise in peace.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Gentleness is not found in anything except that it beautifies it, and it is not removed from anything except that it disgraces it.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: The believer does not taunt, curse, speak obscenely, or speak rudely.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: Whoever relieves a believer's distress, Allah will relieve his distress on the Day of Resurrection.
author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

text: People are like mines of gold and silver; the best of them in the pre-Islamic period are the best of them in Islam, when they have understanding.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Patience is of two kinds: patience over what pains you, and patience against what you covet.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: The tongue is a lion. If you let it loose, it will wound someone.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: A fool's mind is at the mercy of his tongue, and a wise man's tongue is under the control of his mind.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Do not let your difficulties fill you with anxiety; after all, it is in the darkest nights that stars shine more brightly.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Knowledge is better than wealth. Knowledge guards you, while you guard wealth.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: He who has a thousand friends has not a friend to spare, and he who has one enemy will meet him everywhere.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: The best form of devotion to the service of Allah is not to make a show of it.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Detachment is not that you should own nothing, but that nothing should own you.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Do not raise your children the way your parents raised you; they were born for a different time.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: A man's worth depends upon the nobility of his aspirations.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Beautiful patience is patience without complaint.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: The one who admonishes you cares for you, and the one who flatters you deceives you.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Two things define you: your patience when you have nothing and your attitude when you have everything.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: The disease of the heart is worse than the disease of the body.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Silence is the best reply to a fool.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: The value of a man is in what he gives, not in what he is capable of taking.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Associate with people of virtue; you will become one of them. Keep away from people of vice; you will be safeguarded.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Live amongst people in such a manner that if you die they weep over you and if you are alive they crave your company.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: If you want to know where your heart is, look where your mind goes when it wanders.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: The word of justice spoken before an unjust ruler is the best of jihad.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: How strange is the man who destroys his hereafter for his worldly life, or destroys his worldly life for his hereafter, or destroys both for the sake of anger.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Fear only Allah and hope only in Allah.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: He who busies himself with things other than improvement of his own self becomes perplexed.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Stubbornness destroys good advice.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Greed is permanent slavery.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: The highest degree of wisdom is self-knowledge.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Do not be a slave to others when Allah has created you free.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Your remedy is within you, but you do not sense it. Your sickness is from you, but you do not perceive it.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: There is no wealth like knowledge, and no poverty like ignorance.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: The most happy is he to whom Allah has given a good wife.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: He who forgives, and is reconciled unto his enemy, shall receive his reward from Allah.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: A true friend is one who sees a fault, gives you advice, and defends you in your absence.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: The strongest among you is the one who controls his anger.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: Authority does not change a man; it only reveals him.
author: Ali ibn Abi Talib

text: If you are greeted then return the greeting more warmly. If you are favored, then repay the favor generously.
author: Abu Bakr As-Siddiq

text: Good actions are a guard against the blows of adversity.
author: Abu Bakr As-Siddiq

text: Without knowledge, action is useless, and without action, knowledge is futile.
author: Abu Bakr As-Siddiq

text: Be good to others, that will protect you against evil.
author: Abu Bakr As-Siddiq

text: Death is the easiest of all things after it, and the hardest of all things before it.
author: Abu Bakr As-Siddiq

text: The more knowledge you have, the greater will be your fear of Allah.
author: Abu Bakr As-Siddiq

text: He who avoids complaint invites happiness.
author: Abu Bakr As-Siddiq

text: Do not follow vain desires, for verily he who prospers is preserved from lust, greed, and anger.
author: Umar ibn Al-Khattab

text: The less unnecessary talk you engage in, the more wisdom you shall have.
author: Umar ibn Al-Khattab

text: Hold yourself accountable before you are held accountable.
author: Umar ibn Al-Khattab

text: God bless the man who says less and does more.
author: Umar ibn Al-Khattab

text: I have never regretted my silence. As for my speech, I have regretted it many times.
author: Umar ibn Al-Khattab

text: Get used to a rough life, for luxury does not last forever.
author: Umar ibn Al-Khattab

text: He who does not live in the way of his beliefs starts to believe in the way he lives.
author: Umar ibn Al-Khattab

text: If patience and gratitude had been two she-camels, it would matter little on which I ride.
author: Umar ibn Al-Khattab

text: Learn the Arabic language; it strengthens the mind and increases virtue.
author: Umar ibn Al-Khattab

text: The best way to defeat someone is to beat him at politeness.
author: Umar ibn Al-Khattab

text: Verily Allah will help those who are truthful and will destroy those who are liars.
author: Uthman ibn Affan

text: If our hearts were truly pure, we would never have enough of the Word of Allah.
author: Uthman ibn Affan

text: The best of wealth is a thankful heart.
author: Uthman ibn Affan

text: An opinion from a wise man is better than a certainty from a fool.
author: Imam Abu Hanifa

text: If scholars are not the friends of Allah, then Allah has no friends.
author: Imam Abu Hanifa

text: I refute my opinion for the opinion of the one who brings stronger evidence.
author: Imam Abu Hanifa

text: When a hadith is found to be authentic, then that is my school of thought.
author: Imam Abu Hanifa

text: Beware of speaking about Allah without knowledge.
author: Imam Abu Hanifa

text: The one who does not know the difference of the scholars has not smelled the scent of knowledge.
author: Imam Abu Hanifa

text: Do not look at the smallness of the sin but look at the greatness of the One you are disobeying.
author: Imam Abu Hanifa

text: Do not say anything about Allah without knowledge.
author: Imam Malik

text: The shield of the scholar is saying I do not know.
author: Imam Malik

text: Knowledge does not consist in narrating much. Knowledge is a light that Allah places in the heart.
author: Imam Malik

text: Everyone's opinion can be accepted or rejected, except the one in this grave — meaning the Prophet ﷺ.
author: Imam Malik

text: Hastiness in giving verdicts on matters of religion is a type of ignorance and foolishness.
author: Imam Malik

text: Every claim of knowledge must be supported by action, otherwise it is a mere claim.
author: Imam Malik

text: Allah has not created anything He dislikes more than ignorance.
author: Imam Malik

text: I never debated anyone with the intention of defeating him. I only debated to arrive at the truth.
author: Imam Ash-Shafi'i

text: Be hard on yourself, easy on others.
author: Imam Ash-Shafi'i

text: My heart is at ease knowing that what was meant for me will never miss me, and what misses me was never meant for me.
author: Imam Ash-Shafi'i

text: The one who cannot bear the fatigue of learning will have to bear the pain of ignorance.
author: Imam Ash-Shafi'i

text: I wish people could learn this knowledge without it being attributed to me.
author: Imam Ash-Shafi'i

text: Knowledge is not memorization but a light Allah casts into the heart.
author: Imam Ash-Shafi'i

text: If you are unable to do good to people, then at the very least do not harm them.
author: Imam Ash-Shafi'i

text: Strong is the one who overcomes himself, and weak is the one whom his desires overcome.
author: Imam Ash-Shafi'i

text: The scholars are the heirs of the prophets.
author: Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal

text: People have more need of knowledge than they have of food and drink.
author: Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal

text: Be patient. Patience with people is faith.
author: Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal

text: The grave is the first stage of the hereafter; whoever is saved from it, whatever comes after is easier.
author: Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal

text: The people are in greater need of knowledge than of food and drink, for a person needs food and drink two or three times a day, but needs knowledge with every breath.
author: Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal

text: The foundation of knowledge is the fear of Allah.
author: Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal

text: Whoever claims that there is no need for scholars has gone far astray.
author: Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal

text: If you see a person busy with finding faults in others, know that he is rotting from within.
author: Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal

text: Knowledge without action is madness, and action without knowledge is void.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: The happiness of the drop is to die in the river.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: Half of disbelief in Allah in the world is caused by people who make religion look ugly.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: Do not fix your heart on anything but Allah.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: The real test of good manners is in your dealings with those who have bad manners.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: To get what you love, you must first be patient with what you hate.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: A man of bad character punishes his own soul.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: Desires make slaves out of kings and patience makes kings out of slaves.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: The way to paradise is an uphill climb whereas hell is downhill. Hence, there is a struggle to get to paradise and no struggle to get to hell.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: If those who do not possess knowledge avoid the scholarly discussions, disagreement will end.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: Never have I dealt with anything more difficult than my own soul, which sometimes helps me and sometimes opposes me.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: Each of your breaths is a priceless jewel, since each of them is irreplaceable.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: The heart is like a mirror. When sins surround it, the mirror becomes dark.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: Pride in knowledge is among the greatest obstacles to truth.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: Work for your terrestrial life in proportion to your location in it, and work for your afterlife in proportion to your eternity in it.
author: Imam Al-Ghazali

text: Leave the argumentation and dispute in religion, for indeed I have found it to corrupt the hearts.
author: Imam An-Nawawi

text: The station of gratitude is the highest of stations.
author: Imam An-Nawawi

text: Whoever acts upon what he knows, Allah will teach him what he does not know.
author: Imam An-Nawawi

text: Beneficial knowledge is that which causes the heart to fear Allah.
author: Imam An-Nawawi

text: Hold fast to sincerity, for if the sincere one were to be considered a show-off, it would not harm him.
author: Imam An-Nawawi

text: Consistency in small deeds is more beloved to Allah than occasional great deeds.
author: Imam An-Nawawi

text: The scholars said: the one who seeks knowledge must be humble and must leave arrogance behind.
author: Imam An-Nawawi

text: Knowledge is the life of the heart, the light of the eyes from blindness.
author: Imam An-Nawawi

text: A person should never be too proud to ask about that which he does not know.
author: Imam An-Nawawi

text: The foundation of all foundations is sincerity.
author: Imam An-Nawawi

text: Wasting time is worse than death, because death separates you from this world whereas wasting time separates you from Allah.
author: Ibn Al-Jawzi

text: The heart will rest and feel relief if it is settled with Allah. And it will worry and feel anxious if it is settled with people.
author: Ibn Al-Jawzi

text: Whoever desires to purify his heart, then let him prefer Allah to his desires.
author: Ibn Al-Jawzi

text: Do not let the length of the journey scare you; every step brings you closer.
author: Ibn Al-Jawzi

text: Reflect on the fact that the sun rises and sets on you while you are heedless.
author: Ibn Al-Jawzi

text: The one who finds benefit in silence finds safety, and the one who finds benefit in speech finds reward if he says the right thing.
author: Ibn Al-Jawzi

text: Beware of procrastinating. It is the greatest of the soldiers of Satan.
author: Ibn Al-Jawzi

text: He whose days pass without worship has been duped by hope.
author: Ibn Al-Jawzi

text: The devil does not bother to mislead the heedless; they are already lost.
author: Ibn Al-Jawzi

text: If you do not prepare today, you will not be ready tomorrow.
author: Ibn Al-Jawzi

text: The heart was created to remember Allah, so when it is neglectful of His remembrance, it goes astray.
author: As-Suyuti

text: Every science has its elite, and the elite of every science are those who act upon it.
author: As-Suyuti

text: The most noble form of knowledge is that which leads to action.
author: As-Suyuti

text: There is no blessing greater than understanding religion correctly.
author: As-Suyuti

text: He who seeks knowledge for other than Allah will be veiled from the truth.
author: As-Suyuti

text: Whoever turns away from nonsensical talk will find wisdom settling in his heart.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: The heart finds no rest except in the remembrance of Allah.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: The one who is imprisoned is not the one whose body is confined, but the one whose heart is locked away from Allah.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: What can my enemies do to me? My paradise is in my heart, it is with me wherever I go.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: If you do not taste the sweetness of an action in your heart, suspect it, for the Lord is grateful.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: Don't depend too much on anyone in this world because even your own shadow leaves you in darkness.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: Patience is obligatory when there is no choice, and voluntary patience is better.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: People worship Allah in three ways: some worship hoping for something, some worship out of fear, and some worship out of love. The last is the best.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: Sincerity is that the servant's deeds outwardly and inwardly are the same.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: Every punishment from Allah is pure justice, and every blessing from Allah is pure grace.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: The more you run away from the truth, the more you are drawn toward falsehood.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: A slave stands before Allah twice: once in prayer, and once on the Day of Judgment. Whoever fulfills the first standing will find the second easier.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: In the heart there is a hardness which cannot be softened except by remembrance of Allah.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: The heart is like a bird: love is its head and its two wings are hope and fear.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: Verily, in the body there is a piece of flesh. If it is sound, the whole body is sound. If it is corrupt, the whole body is corrupt. Truly it is the heart.
author: Ibn Taymiyyah

text: The heart, in its journey to Allah, is like a bird: love is its head, and fear and hope are its two wings.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: Had Allah lifted the veil for His slave and shown him how He handles his affairs, the slave would have drowned in gratitude.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: Patience and gratitude are twins; they are inseparable.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: Truly in the heart there is a void that cannot be filled except with the company of Allah.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: Sins are like chains and locks preventing their perpetrator from roaming the vast garden of monotheism.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: A person's tongue can give you the taste of his heart.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: The one who loves dunya will be harmed by three things: lasting worry, unending toil, and hopes that are never fulfilled.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: Every drop of sweat and every breath we take in life, if not taken for the sake of Allah, will lead to regret and sorrow on the Day of Judgment.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: If a gem falls into mud it is still valuable. If dust ascends to heaven it remains valueless.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: The du'a made at tahajjud is like an arrow that does not miss its target.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: The most difficult battle is the battle against yourself.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: The greatest cause of heedlessness is lack of reflection.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: Repel the thought, for if you don't, it becomes an idea. Repel the idea, for if you don't, it becomes a desire.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: Abandoning sin is harder than maintaining consistent obedience, so struggle with yourself.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: This world is like a shadow. Run after it and you will never catch it. Turn your back against it and it has no choice but to follow you.
author: Ibn Qayyim Al-Jawziyyah

text: This world and the Hereafter in the heart of a person are like two scales: when one goes up, the other goes down.
author: Hasan Al-Basri

text: O son of Adam, you are nothing but a number of days, whenever each day passes then part of you has gone.
author: Hasan Al-Basri

text: The laughter of a sinner is crying on the inside.
author: Hasan Al-Basri

text: A believer is a mirror to his brother. If he sees something wrong, he corrects it.
author: Hasan Al-Basri

text: Beware of procrastination for you are living in the present, not the future.
author: Hasan Al-Basri

text: Worldly things are not the objective of a wise person. So do not let them be your main concern.
author: Hasan Al-Basri

text: The intelligent one is he who controls his desire and works for what comes after death.
author: Hasan Al-Basri

text: A man may have done good deeds for seventy years and be unjust in his judgment at the end, and so enter the Fire.
author: Hasan Al-Basri

text: If you want to know what you are worth to Allah, see what you are doing with your time.
author: Hasan Al-Basri

text: Faith is not by embellishment or wishful thinking, but it is what is rooted in the heart and proven by action.
author: Hasan Al-Basri

text: Let the days do what they will, and be courageous when fate decrees.
author: Imam Ash-Shafi'i

text: Knowledge is that which benefits, not that which is memorized.
author: Sufyan al-Thawri

text: Do not be concerned about others' opinions of you. Be concerned about your standing with Allah.
author: Sufyan al-Thawri

text: Raise your words, not your voice. It is rain that grows flowers, not thunder.
author: Rumi

text: Yesterday I was clever, so I wanted to change the world. Today I am wise, so I am changing myself.
author: Rumi

text: The wound is the place where the light enters you.
author: Rumi

text: Silence is the language of God. All else is poor translation.
author: Rumi

text: Be grateful for whoever comes, because each has been sent as a guide from beyond.
author: Rumi

text: Knowledge is of two kinds: that which is absorbed and that which is merely heard, and that which is merely heard is of no benefit.
author: Sufyan al-Thawri

text: What has he found who has lost Allah? And what has he lost who has found Allah?
author: Ibn Ata'illah

text: One of the signs of relying on deeds is loss of hope when a downfall occurs.
author: Ibn Ata'illah

text: Knowledge is not what is memorized. Knowledge is what benefits.
author: Imam Ash-Shafi'i
